name: GLYPH FIELD - cursor-proximity variable-font specimen grid
ref: Exat Typeface microsite by HotType (CSSDA WOTY 2025 nominee, Codrops case study April 2026 - tympanus.net/codrops/2026/04/10/the-exat-microsite); Obys Agency kinetic typography (metabole.studio immersive 2026 round-up)
palette: #0d0d0d (near-black) + #f0ece4 (bone glyph fill); proximity rings run ice-blue #90c8f0 at distance to deep red #c0392b at pointer
brief: The entire first viewport is a dense grid of repeated lowercase glyphs set in a single variable font. As the cursor moves, glyphs within 7 concentric distance-rings shift their weight axis and hue in real time - hairline at the periphery, ultra-black at the cursor's center - creating a cursor-shaped pressure depression in the text field. Works are hidden beneath the glyph layer and revealed by clicking a glyph cluster, which parts the field like a curtain. This is distinct from word-level kinetic type (morph): it operates at individual-glyph scale with Euclidean distance math, no animation timeline. Mobile: device tilt (DeviceOrientation) drives the ring center instead of the cursor; works are revealed by tap-and-hold. Performance guardrail: glyph positions are in a fixed DOM grid updated by CSS custom properties per cell via a single rAF loop; no WebGL required.

name: BLIND WIPE - SVG mask reveal gallery (4 wipe patterns)
ref: Codrops 'SVG Mask Transitions on Scroll with GSAP and ScrollTrigger' (March 11 2026 - tympanus.net/codrops/2026/03/11/svg-mask-transitions); Cyd Stumpel Portfolio 2025 CSS View Transitions (Awwwards SOTD March 2025)
palette: #f4f0e8 (bone reveal surface) + #111111 (mask black) + #d8482a (vermilion seam accent visible mid-transition)
brief: Each work in the gallery is hidden behind a full-bleed SVG mask; scrolling to it triggers one of four wipe patterns: (1) horizontal blinds - paired rectangles open from center lines up and down; (2) random grid - rectangular panels open at shuffled timings; (3) vertical blinds - strips expand outward left and right like a venetian blind; (4) column wave - a left-to-right wave with random panel openings within each column. Works cycle through all four patterns as the viewer scrolls down. At scroll=0 the first work is mid-wipe already so the signature mechanic is immediately visible. Distinct from glitch-datamosh (RGB/VHS aesthetic) and fold-origami (paper fold geometry). Mobile: all four patterns work identically on touch-scroll via IntersectionObserver. Guardrail: SVG masks are hardware-accelerated; no canvas or WebGL needed; reduced-motion collapses to instant opacity reveal.

name: IMAGE TUBE - scroll-driven infinite 3D image cylinder
ref: Codrops 'Reactive Depth: Scroll-Driven 3D Image Tube with React Three Fiber' (Feb 17 2026 - tympanus.net/codrops/2026/02/17/reactive-depth-building-a-scroll-driven-3d-image-tube); Lusion studio Three.js inertia + Z-axis depth (lusion.co)
palette: #050505 (void) + #c8f53e (lime accent for UI chrome); images supply all interior color
brief: Works are arranged as image planes on the outer face of a 3D cylinder using trigonometry (each plane faces outward). Scrolling adds rotational velocity to the cylinder; releasing lets inertia carry it and dampen. Hovering slows the entire system. The front-facing plane is the only one fully sharp and lit; side planes curve away in perspective blur. An infinite seamless loop resets the cylinder's Y position at the halfway mark so there is no hard stop. The background hosts a deforming grid plane (vertex shader, smoothstep edge/center mask) that ripples with mouse position. Distinct from ring-carousel (CSS preserve-3d fan, no WebGL) and coverflow-3d (flat cards on Y-axis, no inertia or deformation). Mobile: touch-drag spin replaces scroll; auto-slow on touch-end. WebGL note: avoid GLSL ES 1.0 dynamic-indexed local arrays (ANGLE bug returns 0); use texture LUTs instead; confirm canvas paints before starting the render loop. Guardrail: render at DPR 1; post-process blur is a single-pass shader, not per-element CSS.

name: MOOD METER - scroll speed live-tunes ambient hue and blur
ref: Codrops 'Scroll-Reactive 3D Gallery with Three.js, Velocity, and Mood-Based Backgrounds' (March 9 2026 - tympanus.net/codrops/2026/03/09/building-a-scroll-reactive-3d-gallery); Terminal Industries REJOUICE viral scroll-speed speedometer (threads.net/@framer, Awwwards SOTM Sep 2025)
palette: #0a0a0a (base void); hue shifts dynamically: slow-scroll = icy cyan #a8d4f5, medium = amber #f4a000, fast = magenta #ff2d78; gallery cards stay near-white
brief: A velocity-reading system measures the derivative of scroll position each frame and maps it to a CSS custom property that drives the page's ambient hue, the backdrop blur radius around gallery cards, and a live 'ENERGY' HUD gauge in the corner (thin arc + numeric BPM-style readout). Scrolling slowly keeps the palette cold and cards crisp; scrolling fast turns the background hot and cards hazy. Each work card is a frosted slab whose label weight also tracks velocity. Works are presented in a clean vertical gallery; the mood system is the differentiation, not the layout. At scroll=0 the HUD is live and the hue is cold, revealing the mechanic immediately. Distinct from arc (fixed palette per chapter) and time-of-day (clock-driven, not velocity-driven). Mobile: scroll velocity on touch behaves identically. Guardrail: velocity calc is a single rAF differential; no WebGL; all color transitions are CSS transitions on a custom property.

name: WEBTOON - vertical comic-panel scroll where panels breathe
ref: Ponpon Mania by Patrick Heng / OKAY DEV (Awwwards SOTM October 2025, Codrops case study Oct 7 2025 - tympanus.net/codrops/2025/10/07/ponpon-mania); Siena Film Foundation filmstrip-slider by Niccolo Miranda (Awwwards SOTD March 2025)
palette: #fefcf6 (panel paper) + #1a1a1a (ink outline) + #e63946 (spot red accent for dialogue bubbles and action lines)
brief: Works are presented as vertical comic panels - each panel has an inked SVG border, a caption box in the upper-left corner (work title/prompt), and dialogue bubble annotations. As each panel enters the viewport it 'breathes': subtle CSS scale pulse, a speed-line radial sweep on IntersectionObserver, and the caption types itself in. The gutter between panels is intentionally white with a thin rule - the pacing gap. Clicking a panel expands it manga-style (the panel border scales to fill the viewport with a perspective push). At scroll=0 the top panel is already breathing, revealing the mechanic in the first second. Distinct from manifesto-essay (prose column, inline figures) and zine-collage (cut-paste punk aesthetic with tape and torn paper). Mobile: single-column vertical strip matches the native webtoon format exactly. Guardrail: all panel animations are CSS + IntersectionObserver; zero WebGL; reduced-motion disables the pulse and speed-lines only.

name: JOURNEY MAP - scroll draws a cinematic SVG path across a stylized map
ref: Codrops 'Creating Scroll-Driven SVG Map Animations with GSAP' (May 21 2026 - tympanus.net/codrops/2026/05/21/creating-scroll-driven-svg-map-animations); Floema Jewelry WebGL horizontal+vertical scroll hybrid (Awwwards SOTM May 2026 / burocratik)
brief: The hero is a full-viewport stylized SVG map (hand-drawn contour lines, minimalist place names in a condensed serif). A dashed parchment route line draws itself via stroke-dashoffset as the user scrolls; a small animated 'vehicle' dot follows the path tip. At each work's waypoint the camera zooms into a golden marker using a CSS transform scale, and the work expands from the marker like an unfolded document. The entire narrative is spatial: the visitor is travelling through a creative atlas. Distinct from particle-constellation (star node graph, canvas pan-zoom, no path or map metaphor) and infinite-canvas (spatial board with no narrative direction). Mobile: the map is SVG-responsive; the camera zoom uses transform-origin set to each waypoint; pinch-zoom is disabled to preserve the scroll-driven narrative. Guardrail: all animation is GSAP ScrollTrigger on SVG attributes; no WebGL; the SVG is inline for DOM access.

name: UNFOLD GRID - sticky viewport unlocks cells progressively as you scroll
ref: Codrops 'Sticky Grid Scroll' (March 2 2026 - tympanus.net/Tutorials/StickyGridScroll); Dropbox Brand site (CSSDA WOTY 2025 nominee, staggered-grid reflow)
palette: #f3efe8 (bone) + #111 (ink) + #2c6fad (unlock blue accent for the reveal flash)
brief: The page has a single sticky layout container that fills the viewport and never scrolls away. Below the fold is a long scroll container whose progress drives a single CSS custom property. As that property increases, grid cells transition one-by-one from locked (blurred, desaturated, a padlock icon overlay) to unlocked (sharp, full color, padlock dissolves with a brief blue flash). The 'unlocking' order is non-linear - it spirals from center-outward, creating a growing circle of revealed work. When all cells are unlocked the layout un-sticks and scrolls normally into a detail panel. At scroll=0 all cells are locked except the center one (flashing), immediately demonstrating the mechanic. Distinct from morphing-grid (same cells reflow between formations) and bento (always fully visible). Mobile: 2-column grid; cells unlock top-to-bottom in reading order. Guardrail: lock state is a CSS class toggled by a single scroll listener; no canvas; blur is CSS backdrop-filter with GPU compositing.

name: LIQUID GLASS - frosted translucent panes in true z-depth parallax
ref: Apple Liquid Glass design language (WWDC 2026, widely cited as 2026 design paradigm shift); Oryzo AI by Lusion - Z-axis depth + orbiting camera (Awwwards SOTM April 2026, lusion.co/projects/oryzo_ai)
palette: #0d0d0f (near-black base) + #e8f4ff (ice-white glass tint); blur and layered shadow are the entire color system - no solid fills
brief: Three to five translucent glass panes float at explicit z-depths over a slowly drifting mesh-gradient background. Each pane hosts one work card with backdrop-filter blur creating genuine depth hierarchy - foreground panes are sharp, background panes are heavy-blur. Scrolling moves foreground panes faster than background ones (true parallax, not CSS perspective tricks). Hovering a pane sharpens its blur to near-zero and scales it forward 4px on the Z axis. The design IS the depth system: no solid fills, no borders, just blur delta and layered box-shadow. Distinct from parallax-depth (opaque diorama layers, no frosted glass) and space (WebGL 3D fly-through). Mobile: single-pane stack with swipe-to-advance; blur radius halved for GPU budget. Guardrail: cap at 3 concurrent backdrop-filter elements; paint-contain on each pane; skip effect entirely behind prefers-reduced-motion.

name: PRESSURE - cursor speed drives variable-font weight axis live
ref: Obys Agency kinetic type system (metabole.studio immersive 2026 round-up); AVA SRG metallic bold-typography site (topcssgallery.com 2026 award trends); Envato 2026 'kinetic type: scroll-driven variable weight' trend report
palette: #f8f8f5 (off-white) + #111111 (near-black); zero decorative color - typography alone carries all visual weight
brief: The entire layout is large variable-font headings and labels only. The font-weight axis (100-900) is driven by cursor speed: fast mouse movement pushes weight to 900 (ultra-bold), resting cursor relaxes to 100 (hairline). The width axis responds to scroll velocity: fast scroll = condensed, slow = extended. Works are revealed as the headings expand - each heading masks a work image via CSS clip-path, so the text literally contains the work. Clicking any heading zooms the work to full screen with a weight-transition that normalizes to 400 as the detail view opens. At scroll=0 the cursor speed effect is already live on the hero heading, demonstrating the mechanic before any scroll. Distinct from morph (word-level transforms, no variable axis, no speed mapping) and proximity-glyph-field (glyph-grid at character scale). Mobile: weight responds to touch-scroll speed; width axis ties to scroll velocity on touch. Guardrail: all rendering is pure CSS font-variation-settings updated once per rAF; no canvas, no WebGL.

name: ENDLESS LOOP - seamless infinite parallax scroll with no page end
ref: Codrops 'The Never Ending Story: Seamless Infinite Scroll with GSAP and Lenis' (May 28 2026 - tympanus.net/codrops/2026/05/28/the-never-ending-story); Codrops 'Building an Infinite Parallax Grid with GSAP and Seamless Tiling' (June 2025)
palette: #06060a (deep void) + #ff6b35 (ember accent for loop indicator); works supply all midground color
brief: There is no page end. Works float as framed panels at three depth layers (foreground, midground, background) that each tile seamlessly: when the last item in any layer exits the viewport, it teleports to the front - invisible to the user - creating a perpetual orbit. A subtle 'LOOP' counter in the corner increments each full cycle, the only signal that the scroll is circular. Snap-control gives the experience rhythm at each midground work. The experience is designed for a small curated set (6-12 works) that rewards re-encounter. Distinct from parallax-depth (finite diorama with a scroll endpoint) and infinite-canvas (spatial board you pan freely, not a looping scroll lane). Mobile: touch-scroll drives the same three-layer tiling; loop counter still shown. Guardrail: the tiling logic is a single rAF translate loop using modulo arithmetic on a CSS transform; no duplicated DOM nodes beyond the minimum tile set; no WebGL required.
